rulings.Case Title: OLIVER ARMSTRONG VS. FLYWHEEL TAXI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Declaring Undertaking Insufficient / Objection To Undertaking
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, January 13, 2026, line 8, PLAINTIFF OLIVER ARMSTRONG'S Motion For Order Declaring Undertaking Insufficient / Objection To Undertaking Hearing required. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WINDELER, LLC VS. TOPA INSURANCE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File A Cross-Complaint By Defendant Topa Insurance Company
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, January 13, 2026, line 1, DEFENDANT TOPA INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File A Cross-Complaint By Defendant Topa Insurance Company Defendant Topa Insurance Company's motion for leave to file a cross-complaint is granted in part. Topa is granted leave to file a cross-complaint alleging a single cause of action for declaratory relief regarding rescission of the policy it issued to plaintiff Windeler, LLC. That claim is a compulsory cross-claim and Topa has not acted in bad faith as to that claim so it is not precluded from alleging the claim, especially since the claim does not materially differ from several of Topa's affirmative defenses. The court exercises its discretion to deny Topa's request to allege claims against Novita Insurance Solutions, LLC and Patricia Lowther because those claims are not compulsory and allowing them would almost certainly result in a significant delay of the trial. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address = (301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: COOKIES RETAIL, LLC, VS. COOKIES CREATIVE CONSULTING & PROMOTIONS, LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Responses By Judgment Debtor To Post Judgment Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, January 13, 2026, line 2, DEFENDANT COOKIES CREATIVE CONSULTING & PROMOTIONS, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY Motion To Compel Responses By Judgment Debtor To Post Judgment Discovery Judgment creditor CCC&P's motion to compel responses by judgement debtor CRE to post judgment discovery is denied. This motion is limited to whether CRE waived all of its objections to the discovery and does not address whether any of those objections were validly asserted or whether CRE's should be required to provide further responses to the discovery. CRE has shown that it is entitled to relief from waiver of its objections per CCP 2030.290(a) because its two days late filed responses were in substantial compliance with the applicable code sections and its failure to serve timely responses was the result of mistake, inadvertence and excusable neglect. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MIDLAND CREDIT MANAGEMENT, INC. _ VS. CHRISTINE CARR ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, January 13, 2026, line 4, DEFENDANT CHRISTINE CARR'S Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents Defendant Christine Carr's motion to compel responses to special interrogatories and document requests and for monetary sanctions is granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown for all relief sought by the motion. Plaintiff must serve verified code-compliant responses to all of the interrogatories and documents requests without objections and produce all responsive documents and pay $1,180 in reasonable monetary sanctions no later than February 11, 2026. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: LARRY VAN DEN HEUVEL VS. MADELINE ANGELA BERG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, January 13, 2026, line 6, DEFENDANT MADELINE BERG MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT Defendant Madeline Berg's motion to strike the punitive damages allegations in the complaint filed by plaintiff Larry Van Den Heuvel is granted without prejudice to plaintiff seeking leave to file an amended complaint if and when in good faith he is able to allege additional ultimate facts supporting punitive damages. The mere allegation that defendant was looking at her cell phone which caused an accident is insufficient. No opposition filed. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom.
